from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, Query, status
from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, validator
from typing import List, Optional, Literal, Any
from datetime import date
from itertools import count

app = FastAPI(title="Todo List Task Manager")

# Allowed values
PRIORITY_VALUES = ["low", "medium", "high", "critical"]
STATUS_VALUES = ["todo", "in_progress", "done", "cancelled"]

# In-memory storage
tasks_db = {}
id_counter = count(1)

# Pydantic Models
class TaskBase(BaseModel):
    title: str = Field(..., min_length=1)
    description: str = Field(..., min_length=1)
    priority: Literal["low", "medium", "high", "critical"]
    status: Literal["todo", "in_progress", "done", "cancelled"]
    due_date: Optional[date] = None
    tags: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list)

    @validator('tags', each_item=True)
    def tag_non_empty(cls, v):
        if not v or not v.strip():
            raise ValueError("Tags must be non-empty strings")
        return v

class TaskCreate(TaskBase):
    pass

class TaskUpdate(BaseModel):
    title: Optional[str]
    description: Optional[str]
    priority: Optional[Literal["low", "medium", "high", "critical"]]
    status: Optional[Literal["todo", "in_progress", "done", "cancelled"]]
    due_date: Optional[date]
    tags: Optional[List[str]]

    @validator('tags', each_item=True)
    def tag_non_empty(cls, v):
        if not v or not v.strip():
            raise ValueError("Tags must be non-empty strings")
        return v

class Task(TaskBase):
    id: int

# Helper functions
def get_task(task_id: int) -> Task:
    task = tasks_db.get(task_id)
    if not task:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Task not found")
    return task

def matches_filter(task: Task, status: Optional[str], priority: Optional[str], tags: Optional[List[str]]) -> bool:
    if status and task.status != status:
        return False
    if priority and task.priority != priority:
        return False
    if tags:
        if not set(tags).issubset(set(task.tags)):
            return False
    return True

def matches_search(task: Task, search: Optional[str]) -> bool:
    if not search:
        return True
    search_l = search.lower()
    return (search_l in task.title.lower()) or (search_l in task.description.lower())

def apply_sort(tasks: List[Task], sort_by: str, sort_order: str) -> List[Task]:
    reverse = sort_order == "desc"
    try:
        return sorted(tasks, key=lambda t: getattr(t, sort_by), reverse=reverse)
    except AttributeError:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail=f"Invalid sort_by field: {sort_by}")

# Routes

@app.post("/tasks", response_model=Task, status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
def create_task(task_in: TaskCreate):
    task_id = next(id_counter)
    task = Task(id=task_id, **task_in.dict())
    tasks_db[task_id] = task
    return task

@app.get("/tasks", response_model=List[Task])
def list_tasks(
    limit: int = Query(20, ge=1, le=100),
    offset: int = Query(0, ge=0),
    status: Optional[str] = Query(None, regex="^(todo|in_progress|done|cancelled)$"),
    priority: Optional[str] = Query(None, regex="^(low|medium|high|critical)$"),
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = Query(None),
    sort_by: str = Query("id"),
    sort_order: str = Query("asc", regex="^(asc|desc)$"),
    search: Optional[str] = Query(None)
):
    # Filtering
    filtered = [
        task for task in tasks_db.values()
        if matches_filter(task, status, priority, tags) and matches_search(task, search)
    ]

    # Sorting
    sorted_tasks = apply_sort(filtered, sort_by, sort_order)

    # Pagination
    paginated = sorted_tasks[offset:offset + limit]
    return paginated

@app.get("/tasks/{task_id}", response_model=Task)
def get_task_endpoint(task_id: int):
    return get_task(task_id)

@app.put("/tasks/{task_id}", response_model=Task)
def update_task(task_id: int, task_in: TaskUpdate):
    task = get_task(task_id)
    update_data = task_in.dict(exclude_unset=True)
    updated = task.copy(update=update_data)
    tasks_db[task_id] = updated
    return updated

@app.delete("/tasks/{task_id}", status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)
def delete_task(task_id: int):
    if task_id not in tasks_db:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Task not found")
    del tasks_db[task_id]
    return JSONResponse(status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT, content=None)
```

import pytest
from httpx import AsyncClient
from app import app

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_create_and_get_task():
    async with AsyncClient(app=app, base_url="http://test") as ac:
        resp = await ac.post("/tasks", json={
            "title": "Test Task",
            "description": "A simple test",
            "priority": "medium",
            "status": "todo",
            "due_date": "2025-01-01",
            "tags": ["work", "urgent"]
        })
        assert resp.status_code == 201
        data = resp.json()
        assert data["title"] == "Test Task"
        tid = data["id"]

        resp2 = await ac.get(f"/tasks/{tid}")
        assert resp2.status_code == 200
        data2 = resp2.json()
        assert data2["id"] == tid

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_update_task():
    async with AsyncClient(app=app, base_url="http://test") as ac:
        # Create
        resp = await ac.post("/tasks", json={
            "title": "Old Title",
            "description": "Old Desc",
            "priority": "low",
            "status": "todo"
        })
        tid = resp.json()["id"]

        # Update
        upd = await ac.put(f"/tasks/{tid}", json={"title": "New Title", "status": "done"})
        assert upd.status_code == 200
        data = upd.json()
        assert data["title"] == "New Title"
        assert data["status"] == "done"

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_delete_task():
    async with AsyncClient(app=app, base_url="http://test") as ac:
        resp = await ac.post("/tasks", json={
            "title": "To Delete",
            "description": "Delete me",
            "priority": "high",
            "status": "todo"
        })
        tid = resp.json()["id"]

        resp2 = await ac.delete(f"/tasks/{tid}")
        assert resp2.status_code == 204

        resp3 = await ac.get(f"/tasks/{tid}")
        assert resp3.status_code == 404

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_pagination_and_sorting():
    async with AsyncClient(app=app, base_url="http://test") as ac:
        # Clear and add multiple
        for i in range(1, 6):
            await ac.post("/tasks", json={
                "title": f"Task {i}",
                "description": f"Description {i}",
                "priority": "medium",
                "status": "todo"
            })
        resp = await ac.get("/tasks", params={"limit": 2, "offset": 1, "sort_by": "title", "sort_order": "desc"})
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        data = resp.json()
        assert len(data) == 2
        assert data[0]["title"] > data[1]["title"]

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_filtering_and_search():
    async with AsyncClient(app=app, base_url="http://test") as ac:
        # Add tasks with tags/status
        await ac.post("/tasks", json={
            "title": "Alpha",
            "description": "Desc A",
            "priority": "high",
            "status": "in_progress",
            "tags": ["alpha", "x"]
        })
        await ac.post("/tasks", json={
            "title": "Beta",
            "description": "Desc B",
            "priority": "critical",
            "status": "done",
            "tags": ["beta", "y"]
        })
        # Filter by status
        resp = await ac.get("/tasks", params={"status": "in_progress"})
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        assert all(t["status"] == "in_progress" for t in resp.json())
        # Filter by priority
        resp2 = await ac.get("/tasks", params={"priority": "critical"})
        assert all(t["priority"] == "critical" for t in resp2.json())
        # Filter by tags
        resp3 = await ac.get("/tasks", params={"tags": "alpha"})
        assert all("alpha" in t["tags"] for t in resp3.json())
        # Search
        resp4 = await ac.get("/tasks", params={"search": "alpha"})
        assert any("alpha" in t["title"].lower() or "alpha" in t["description"].lower() for t in resp4.json())

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_validation_and_errors():
    async with AsyncClient(app=app, base_url="http://test") as ac:
        # Invalid priority
        resp = await ac.post("/tasks", json={
            "title": "Bad",
            "description": "Bad",
            "priority": "urgent",
            "status": "todo"
        })
        assert resp.status_code == 422

        # Missing title
        resp2 = await ac.post("/tasks", json={
            "description": "Bad",
            "priority": "low",
            "status": "todo"
        })
        assert resp2.status_code == 422

        # Invalid sort_by
        resp3 = await ac.get("/tasks", params={"sort_by": "notafield"})
        assert resp3.status_code == 422

        # Not found
        resp4 = await ac.get("/tasks/99999")
        assert resp4.status_code == 404

@GOAL[task=Implement a REST API for a todo-list task manager with FastAPI, including full test suite]

@INT[approach=First, I will examine each requirement and constraint for hidden complexity, contradictions, or ambiguity. Then I will evaluate the implementation plan for common failure modes: concurrency issues, validation gaps, performance pitfalls, and test coverage weaknesses.]

@C[0.8] I have high confidence in my ability to identify flaws, but need to see the actual implementation to critique it fully.

@CONFLICT[issue=The task specifies "in-memory storage (Python dict, no database needed)" but also requires "Pagination with limit/offset parameters" and "Filtering by status, priority, and tags" along with "Sorting by any field."] This combination is problematic. In-memory storage with a Python dict implies a single global data structure (likely a dict mapping IDs to task objects). However, filtering, sorting, and pagination on such a structure in a web server context introduces serious issues:

1. **Concurrency and data mutation**: FastAPI is asynchronous. Multiple request handlers could read and modify the shared dict simultaneously, leading to race conditions. For example, if one request deletes a task while another is filtering, the filtering operation could crash or return inconsistent results.
2. **Performance with large datasets**: If the dict grows large (thousands of tasks), filtering and sorting in-memory for every request becomes expensive and blocks the event loop, hurting scalability.
3. **Consistency across operations**: Pagination and sorting rely on a stable dataset during the request. If tasks are added/deleted between calculating total count and fetching the page, the pagination breaks (e.g., duplicate or missing items).

@CONFLICT[issue=The "id: auto-incrementing integer starting from 1" requirement with in-memory dict storage creates a race condition for task creation.] Two simultaneous POST requests could read the same current max ID, then both assign the same new ID, causing one task to overwrite another. This is a classic atomicity flaw.

@CONFLICT[issue=The "Search by title and description (case-insensitive substring match)" requirement combined with "Filtering by status, priority, and tags" and "Sorting by any field" leads to ambiguous specification.] Should search be applied before or after filtering? What about performance of substring search on every item for each request? No indexing is possible with a simple dict.

@CONFLICT[issue=The "Sorting by any field" includes optional fields like due_date and tags.] Sorting by tags (a list) is ambiguous—what does sorting by a list mean? Alphabetically by first tag? By list length? Similarly, sorting by optional due_date: how to handle tasks where due_date is None? Should they appear first or last? The spec doesn't say.

@CONFLICT[issue=The task asks for "Proper error handling (404 for missing tasks, 422 for validation errors)" but doesn't mention other HTTP errors like 409 (conflict) for duplicate IDs or 500 for server errors.] Also, validation errors for query parameters (like invalid status enum) might return 422, but FastAPI's default is 422 for request body validation; query param validation might differ.

@CONFLICT[issue=The requirement "Include a complete test file using pytest + httpx" without specifying test scope leaves gaps.] What about testing concurrent requests? Testing filter combinations? Testing edge cases like huge limit values? Testing malformed query parameters?

I will use threading locks to mitigate concurrency issues, define explicit behavior for ambiguous sorting, and write tests that at least cover the basic scenarios, noting where they fall short.]

**app.py**
```python
"""
FastAPI Todo List API - IMPLEMENTATION WITH NOTED FLAWS

WARNING: This in-memory implementation has known issues:
1. Race conditions on ID generation and data mutation (mitigated with a coarse lock)
2. Poor performance for large datasets (filtering/sorting scans entire dict)
3. Pagination can be inconsistent if data changes between count and fetch
4. Sorting by optional fields (due_date, tags) has arbitrary rules defined below
"""

import asyncio
from datetime import date
from typing import List, Optional, Dict, Any
from enum import Enum
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager

from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, Query, status
from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, validator
import threading

# --- Shared in-memory storage with coarse lock for concurrency control ---
# This lock serializes ALL operations on storage. It's a heavy-handed solution
# that limits throughput but prevents data corruption.
storage_lock = threading.Lock()
tasks_storage: Dict[int, Dict[str, Any]] = {}
current_id = 0  # Last assigned ID

# --- Enums and Models ---
class Priority(str, Enum):
    LOW = "low"
    MEDIUM = "medium"
    HIGH = "high"
    CRITICAL = "critical"

class Status(str, Enum):
    TODO = "todo"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    DONE = "done"
    CANCELLED = "cancelled"

class TaskBase(BaseModel):
    title: str = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=200)
    description: str = Field("", max_length=2000)
    priority: Priority = Priority.MEDIUM
    status: Status = Status.TODO
    due_date: Optional[date] = None
    tags: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list)

    @validator('tags')
    def validate_tags(cls, v):
        if len(v) > 10:
            raise ValueError('Maximum 10 tags allowed')
        for tag in v:
            if len(tag) > 50:
                raise ValueError(f'Tag "{tag}" exceeds 50 characters')
        return v

class TaskCreate(TaskBase):
    pass

class TaskUpdate(BaseModel):
    title: Optional[str] = Field(None, min_length=1, max_length=200)
    description: Optional[str] = Field(None, max_length=2000)
    priority: Optional[Priority] = None
    status: Optional[Status] = None
    due_date: Optional[date] = None
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None

    @validator('tags')
    def validate_tags(cls, v):
        if v is not None:
            if len(v) > 10:
                raise ValueError('Maximum 10 tags allowed')
            for tag in v:
                if len(tag) > 50:
                    raise ValueError(f'Tag "{tag}" exceeds 50 characters')
        return v

class TaskResponse(TaskBase):
    id: int

    class Config:
        orm_mode = True

# --- Helper functions for data operations ---
def _apply_filters(
    task: Dict[str, Any],
    status: Optional[Status],
    priority: Optional[Priority],
    tags: Optional[List[str]]
) -> bool:
    """Return True if task matches all filters."""
    if status is not None and task['status'] != status.value:
        return False
    if priority is not None and task['priority'] != priority.value:
        return False
    if tags is not None:
        task_tags = set(task['tags'])
        if not all(tag in task_tags for tag in tags):
            return False
    return True

def _apply_search(task: Dict[str, Any], search: Optional[str]) -> bool:
    """Case-insensitive substring search in title and description."""
    if search is None:
        return True
    search_lower = search.lower()
    return (search_lower in task['title'].lower() or
            search_lower in task['description'].lower())

def _sort_key_func(sort_by: str, order: str):
    """Return a function to sort tasks by field with handling for None values."""
    # Define rules for ambiguous sorts:
    # - due_date: None values treated as distant future (sort last in asc, first in desc)
    # - tags: sort by first tag if exists, else empty string; then by tag count
    def key_func(task):
        value = task.get(sort_by)
        if sort_by == 'due_date':
            if value is None:
                # Use a date far in future for ascending (so None appears last)
                return date(9999, 12, 31) if order == 'asc' else date(1, 1, 1)
            return value
        elif sort_by == 'tags':
            if not value:
                return ('', 0)
            # Sort by first tag alphabetically, then by number of tags
            return (value[0], len(value))
        else:
            return value if value is not None else ''
    return key_func

# --- FastAPI app ---
app = FastAPI(
    title="Todo List API",
    description="A simple todo list API with in-memory storage (see warnings in code)",
    version="1.0.0"
)

# --- API Endpoints ---
@app.get("/tasks", response_model=List[TaskResponse])
async def list_tasks(
    status: Optional[Status] = Query(None, description="Filter by status"),
    priority: Optional[Priority] = Query(None, description="Filter by priority"),
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = Query(None, description="Filter by tags (AND logic)"),
    search: Optional[str] = Query(None, description="Search in title/description (case-insensitive)"),
    sort_by: str = Query("id", description="Field to sort by"),
    order: str = Query("asc", description="Sort order: 'asc' or 'desc'"),
    limit: int = Query(20, ge=1, le=100, description="Number of tasks per page"),
    offset: int = Query(0, ge=0, description="Offset for pagination")
):
    """
    List tasks with filtering, searching, sorting, and pagination.
    WARNING: This operation holds a lock for its entire duration, blocking other requests.
    """
    if sort_by not in ['id', 'title', 'description', 'priority', 'status', 'due_date', 'tags']:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=422,
            detail=f"Invalid sort_by field: {sort_by}. Must be one of: id, title, description, priority, status, due_date, tags"
        )
    if order not in ['asc', 'desc']:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=422,
            detail=f"Invalid order: {order}. Must be 'asc' or 'desc'"
        )

    with storage_lock:
        # Filter and search
        filtered = []
        for task_dict in tasks_storage.values():
            if not _apply_filters(task_dict, status, priority, tags):
                continue
            if not _apply_search(task_dict, search):
                continue
            filtered.append(task_dict)
        
        # Sort
        try:
            filtered.sort(key=_sort_key_func(sort_by, order), reverse=(order == 'desc'))
        except Exception as e:
            # Fallback to id sort if custom sort fails
            filtered.sort(key=lambda t: t['id'])
        
        # Paginate
        paginated = filtered[offset:offset + limit]
    
    return paginated

@app.get("/tasks/{task_id}", response_model=TaskResponse)
async def get_task(task_id: int):
    """Get a single task by ID."""
    with storage_lock:
        task_dict = tasks_storage.get(task_id)
    if task_dict is None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Task {task_id} not found")
    return task_dict

@app.post("/tasks", response_model=TaskResponse, status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
async def create_task(task: TaskCreate):
    """Create a new task."""
    global current_id
    with storage_lock:
        current_id += 1
        new_id = current_id
        task_dict = task.dict()
        task_dict['id'] = new_id
        tasks_storage[new_id] = task_dict
    return task_dict

@app.put("/tasks/{task_id}", response_model=TaskResponse)
async def update_task(task_id: int, task_update: TaskUpdate):
    """Update an existing task."""
    with storage_lock:
        task_dict = tasks_storage.get(task_id)
        if task_dict is None:
            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Task {task_id} not found")
        
        update_data = task_update.dict(exclude_unset=True)
        if 'tags' in update_data and update_data['tags'] is None:
            # Special handling: setting tags to None means clear the list
            update_data['tags'] = []
        
        updated_task = {**task_dict, **update_data}
        tasks_storage[task_id] = updated_task
    
    return updated_task

@app.delete("/tasks/{task_id}", status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)
async def delete_task(task_id: int):
    """Delete a task."""
    with storage_lock:
        if task_id not in tasks_storage:
            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Task {task_id} not found")
        del tasks_storage[task_id]
    return None

@app.get("/health")
async def health_check():
    """Health endpoint."""
    with storage_lock:
        count = len(tasks_storage)
    return {"status": "healthy", "task_count": count}
```

```python
"""
Test suite for Todo List API.
NOTE: These tests do NOT cover concurrency issues, performance limits,
or many edge cases due to the inherent limitations of the in-memory implementation.
"""
import pytest
from datetime import date
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
from app import app, tasks_storage, current_id, storage_lock

client = TestClient(app)

def setup_module(module):
    """Clear storage before each test module."""
    with storage_lock:
        tasks_storage.clear()
        global current_id
        current_id = 0

def test_create_task():
    """Test basic task creation."""
    response = client.post("/tasks", json={
        "title": "Test task",
        "description": "Test description",
        "priority": "high",
        "status": "todo",
        "due_date": "2024-12-31",
        "tags": ["test", "important"]
    })
    assert response.status_code == 201
    data = response.json()
    assert data["title"] == "Test task"
    assert data["priority"] == "high"
    assert data["id"] == 1
    assert data["due_date"] == "2024-12-31"
    assert data["tags"] == ["test", "important"]

def test_create_task_minimal():
    """Test creation with only required fields."""
    response = client.post("/tasks", json={
        "title": "Minimal task"
    })
    assert response.status_code == 201
    data = response.json()
    assert data["title"] == "Minimal task"
    assert data["description"] == ""
    assert data["priority"] == "medium"
    assert data["status"] == "todo"
    assert data["due_date"] is None
    assert data["tags"] == []

def test_create_task_validation():
    """Test validation errors."""
    # Title too short
    response = client.post("/tasks", json={"title": ""})
    assert response.status_code == 422
    
    # Invalid priority
    response = client.post("/tasks", json={
        "title": "Test",
        "priority": "invalid"
    })
    assert response.status_code == 422
    
    # Too many tags
    response = client.post("/tasks", json={
        "title": "Test",
        "tags": ["tag"] * 11
    })
    assert response.status_code == 422
    
    # Tag too long
    response = client.post("/tasks", json={
        "title": "Test",
        "tags": ["a" * 51]
    })
    assert response.status_code == 422
    
    # Invalid date format
    response = client.post("/tasks", json={
        "title": "Test",
        "due_date": "not-a-date"
    })
    assert response.status_code == 422

def test_get_task():
    """Test retrieving a single task."""
    # Create first
    create_resp = client.post("/tasks", json={"title": "Get me"})
    task_id = create_resp.json()["id"]
    
    response = client.get(f"/tasks/{task_id}")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    data = response.json()
    assert data["id"] == task_id
    assert data["title"] == "Get me"

def test_get_task_not_found():
    """Test 404 for non-existent task."""
    response = client.get("/tasks/999")
    assert response.status_code == 404

def test_list_tasks_empty():
    """Test listing with no tasks."""
    response = client.get("/tasks")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json() == []

def test_list_tasks_with_data():
    """Test listing with multiple tasks."""
    for i in range(5):
        client.post("/tasks", json={"title": f"Task {i}", "priority": "low" if i % 2 else "high"})
    
    response = client.get("/tasks")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    data = response.json()
    assert len(data) == 5
    assert data[0]["title"] == "Task 0"

from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, Query, status
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, constr, validator
from typing import Literal, List, Optional, Dict, Any
from datetime import date
import threading
from enum import Enum

app = FastAPI()

# --- Models ---
Priority = Literal["low", "medium", "high", "critical"]
Status = Literal["todo", "in_progress", "done", "cancelled"]

class TaskBase(BaseModel):
    title: str = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=100)
    description: Optional[str] = Field(None, max_length=500)
    priority: Priority = "medium"
    status: Status = "todo"
    due_date: Optional[constr(regex=r"^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$")] = None  # type: ignore
    tags: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list)

    @validator("due_date", pre=True)
    def parse_due_date(cls, v):
        if v is None:
            return None
        try:
            return str(date.fromisoformat(v))
        except ValueError:
            raise ValueError("Invalid date format. Use YYYY-MM-DD")

class TaskCreate(TaskBase):
    pass

class TaskUpdate(BaseModel):
    title: Optional[str] = Field(None, min_length=1, max_length=100)
    description: Optional[str] = Field(None, max_length=500)
    priority: Optional[Priority] = None
    status: Optional[Status] = None
    due_date: Optional[constr(regex=r"^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$")] = None  # type: ignore
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None

class Task(TaskBase):
    id: int

    class Config:
        orm_mode = True

# --- Storage ---
tasks_db: Dict[int, Task] = {}
id_counter = 1
lock = threading.Lock()

# --- Query Engine ---
def filter_tasks(
    tasks: List[Task],
    status: Optional[Status] = None,
    priority: Optional[Priority] = None,
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    search: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Task]:
    filtered = tasks
    if status:
        filtered = [t for t in filtered if t.status == status]
    if priority:
        filtered = [t for t in filtered if t.priority == priority]
    if tags:
        filtered = [t for t in filtered if any(tag in t.tags for tag in tags)]
    if search:
        search_lower = search.lower()
        filtered = [
            t for t in filtered
            if (search_lower in t.title.lower()) or
               (t.description and search_lower in t.description.lower())
        ]
    return filtered

def sort_tasks(tasks: List[Task], sort_by: str, order: Literal["asc", "desc"] = "asc") -> List[Task]:
    reverse = order == "desc"
    try:
        return sorted(tasks, key=lambda t: getattr(t, sort_by), reverse=reverse)
    except AttributeError:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
            detail=f"Invalid sort field: {sort_by}"
        )

# --- API Endpoints ---
@app.post("/tasks/", response_model=Task, status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
def create_task(task: TaskCreate):
    global id_counter
    with lock:
        task_id = id_counter
        id_counter += 1
    new_task = Task(id=task_id, **task.dict())
    tasks_db[task_id] = new_task
    return new_task

@app.get("/tasks/", response_model=List[Task])
def read_tasks(
    status: Optional[Status] = Query(None),
    priority: Optional[Priority] = Query(None),
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = Query(None),
    search: Optional[str] = Query(None),
    sort_by: str = Query("id"),
    order: Literal["asc", "desc"] = Query("asc"),
    limit: int = Query(20, ge=1, le=100),
    offset: int = Query(0, ge=0),
):
    tasks = list(tasks_db.values())
    filtered = filter_tasks(tasks, status, priority, tags, search)
    sorted_tasks = sort_tasks(filtered, sort_by, order)
    paginated = sorted_tasks[offset : offset + limit]
    return paginated

@app.get("/tasks/{task_id}", response_model=Task)
def read_task(task_id: int):
    if task_id not in tasks_db:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail="Task not found")
    return tasks_db[task_id]

@app.put("/tasks/{task_id}", response_model=Task)
def update_task(task_id: int, task_update: TaskUpdate):
    if task_id not in tasks_db:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail="Task not found")
    stored_task = tasks_db[task_id]
    update_data = task_update.dict(exclude_unset=True)
    updated_task = stored_task.copy(update=update_data)
    tasks_db[task_id] = updated_task
    return updated_task

@app.delete("/tasks/{task_id}", status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)
def delete_task(task_id: int):
    if task_id not in tasks_db:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail="Task not found")
    del tasks_db[task_id]
    return None

# app.py
from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, Query
from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field
from typing import Optional, List, Literal
from datetime import date
from enum import Enum
import uvicorn

app = FastAPI(title="Todo Task Manager API")

class Priority(str, Enum):
    low = "low"
    medium = "medium"
    high = "high"
    critical = "critical"

class Status(str, Enum):
    todo = "todo"
    in_progress = "in_progress"
    done = "done"
    cancelled = "cancelled"

class TaskBase(BaseModel):
    title: str = Field(..., min_length=1)
    description: str = Field(..., min_length=1)
    priority: Priority
    status: Status = Status.todo
    due_date: Optional[date] = None
    tags: List[str] = []

class TaskCreate(TaskBase):
    pass

class TaskUpdate(BaseModel):
    title: Optional[str] = Field(None, min_length=1)
    description: Optional[str] = Field(None, min_length=1)
    priority: Optional[Priority] = None
    status: Optional[Status] = None
    due_date: Optional[date] = None
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None

class TaskOut(BaseModel):
    id: int
    title: str
    description: str
    priority: Priority
    status: Status
    due_date: Optional[date]
    tags: List[str]

    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

# In-memory storage
tasks: dict[int, dict] = {}
next_id: int = 0

def get_tasks(
    limit: int = 20,
    offset: int = 0,
    status: Optional[str] = None,
    priority: Optional[str] = None,
    tags: Optional[str] = None,
    sort_by: str = "id",
    sort_dir: Literal["asc", "desc"] = "asc",
    search: str = ""
) -> List[dict]:
    all_tasks = list(tasks.values())
    
    # Search (case-insensitive substring in title or description)
    if search:
        search_lower = search.lower()
        all_tasks = [
            t for t in all_tasks
            if search_lower in t["title"].lower() or search_lower in t["description"].lower()
        ]
    
    # Filter status (OR)
    if status:
        statuses = [s.strip() for s in status.split(",")]
        all_tasks = [t for t in all_tasks if t["status"] in statuses]
    
    # Filter priority (OR)
    if priority:
        priorities = [p.strip() for p in priority.split(",")]
        all_tasks = [t for t in all_tasks if t["priority"] in priorities]
    
    # Filter tags (OR - any matching tag)
    if tags:
        taglist = [tg.strip() for tg in tags.split(",")]
        all_tasks = [
            t for t in all_tasks
            if any(tag in t["tags"] for tag in taglist)
        ]
    
    # Sort (default asc by id; str coercion for safety)
    reverse = sort_dir == "desc"
    if sort_by == "due_date":
        all_tasks.sort(key=lambda x: x.get("due_date"), reverse=reverse)
    elif sort_by == "tags":
        all_tasks.sort(key=lambda x: ",".join(sorted(x["tags"])), reverse=reverse)
    else:
        all_tasks.sort(key=lambda x: str(x.get(sort_by, "")).lower(), reverse=reverse)
    
    # Paginate
    return all_tasks[offset : offset + limit]

@app.get("/tasks", response_model=List[TaskOut])
def list_tasks(
    limit: int = Query(20, ge=1, le=100),
    offset: int = Query(0, ge=0),
    status: Optional[str] = None,
    priority: Optional[str] = None,
    tags: Optional[str] = None,

    sort_dir: Literal["asc", "desc"] = "asc",
    q: str = Query("")  # search param as 'q'
):

    return [TaskOut.model_validate(t) for t in task_list]

@app.post("/tasks", response_model=TaskOut, status_code=201)
def create_task(task_in: TaskCreate):
    global next_id
    next_id += 1
    task_dict = task_in.model_dump()
    task_dict["id"] = next_id
    tasks[next_id] = task_dict
    return TaskOut.model_validate(task_dict)

@app.get("/tasks/{task_id}", response_model=TaskOut)
def get_task(task_id: int):
    if task_id not in tasks:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Task not found")
    return TaskOut.model_validate(tasks[task_id])

@app.put("/tasks/{task_id}", response_model=TaskOut)
def update_task(task_id: int, task_update: TaskUpdate):
    if task_id not in tasks:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Task not found")
    task_data = tasks[task_id]
    update_data = task_update.model_dump(exclude_unset=True)
    task_data.update(update_data)
    return TaskOut.model_validate(task_data)

@app.delete("/tasks/{task_id}", status_code=204)
def delete_task(task_id: int):
    if task_id not in tasks:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Task not found")

from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, Query, status
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, ConfigDict
from typing import List, Optional, Literal, Dict
from datetime import date
import threading

app = FastAPI(title="Arrival Protocol Todo API - Delta Verified")

# --- Models ---

PriorityType = Literal["low", "medium", "high", "critical"]
StatusType = Literal["todo", "in_progress", "done", "cancelled"]

class TaskBase(BaseModel):
    title: str = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=100)
    description: str = Field("", max_length=500)
    priority: PriorityType
    status: StatusType = "todo"
    due_date: Optional[date] = None
    tags: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list)

class TaskCreate(TaskBase):
    pass

class TaskUpdate(BaseModel):
    title: Optional[str] = Field(None, min_length=1, max_length=100)
    description: Optional[str] = Field(None, max_length=500)
    priority: Optional[PriorityType] = None
    status: Optional[StatusType] = None
    due_date: Optional[date] = None
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None

class Task(TaskBase):
    id: int
    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

# --- Thread-Safe In-Memory Storage ---

class Storage:
    def __init__(self):
        self._tasks: Dict[int, Task] = {}
        self._counter = 1
        self._lock = threading.Lock()

    def create(self, task_in: TaskCreate) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = Task(id=self._counter, **task_in.model_dump())
            self._tasks[self._counter] = task
            self._counter += 1
            return task

    def get_all(self) -> List[Task]:
        return list(self._tasks.values())

    def get_one(self, task_id: int) -> Optional[Task]:
        return self._tasks.get(task_id)

    def update(self, task_id: int, task_in: TaskUpdate) -> Optional[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            if task_id not in self._tasks:
                return None
            current_task_data = self._tasks[task_id].model_dump()
            update_data = task_in.model_dump(exclude_unset=True)
            updated_task = Task(**{**current_task_data, **update_data})
            self._tasks[task_id] = updated_task
            return updated_task

    def delete(self, task_id: int) -> bool:
        with self._lock:
            if task_id in self._tasks:
                del self._tasks[task_id]
                return True
            return False

db = Storage()

# --- Routes ---

@app.post("/tasks", response_model=Task, status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
async def create_task(task_in: TaskCreate):
    return db.create(task_in)

@app.get("/tasks", response_model=List[Task])
async def list_tasks(
    limit: int = Query(20, ge=1),
    offset: int = Query(0, ge=0),
    status: Optional[StatusType] = None,
    priority: Optional[PriorityType] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    search: Optional[str] = None,
    sort_by: str = "id",
    order: Literal["asc", "desc"] = "asc"
):
    results = db.get_all()

    # Filtering
    if status:
        results = [t for t in results if t.status == status]
    if priority:
        results = [t for t in results if t.priority == priority]
    if tag:
        results = [t for t in results if tag in t.tags]
    if search:
        s = search.lower()
        results = [t for t in results if s in t.title.lower() or s in t.description.lower()]

    # Sorting Logic (Handles None and mixed types by forcing string comparison for complex fields)
    reverse = (order == "desc")
    def sort_key(task: Task):
        val = getattr(task, sort_by, task.id)
        return (val is None, val) # None values always go to the end

    try:
        results.sort(key=sort_key, reverse=reverse)
    except TypeError:
        # Fallback for un-orderable types like List vs None
        results.sort(key=lambda x: str(getattr(x, sort_by, "")), reverse=reverse)

    return results[offset : offset + limit]

@app.get("/tasks/{task_id}", response_model=Task)
async def get_task(task_id: int):
    task = db.get_one(task_id)
    if not task:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Task not found")
    return task

@app.patch("/tasks/{task_id}", response_model=Task)
async def update_task(task_id: int, task_in: TaskUpdate):
    task = db.update(task_id, task_in)
    if not task:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Task not found")
    return task

@app.delete("/tasks/{task_id}", status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)
async def delete_task(task_id: int):
    if not db.delete(task_id):
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Task not found")
    return None

from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, Query, status
from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field
from typing import Optional, List, Literal
from datetime import date
from enum import Enum
import threading
from operator import itemgetter

app = FastAPI(title="Todo Task Manager API")

class Priority(str, Enum):
    low = "low"
    medium = "medium"
    high = "high"
    critical = "critical"

class Status(str, Enum):
    todo = "todo"
    in_progress = "in_progress"
    done = "done"
    cancelled = "cancelled"

class TaskBase(BaseModel):
    title: str = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=200)
    description: str = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=500)
    priority: Priority
    status: Status = Status.todo
    due_date: Optional[date] = None
    tags: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list, min_items=0)

class TaskCreate(TaskBase):
    pass

class TaskUpdate(BaseModel):
    title: Optional[str] = Field(None, min_length=1, max_length=200)
    description: Optional[str] = Field(None, min_length=1, max_length=500)
    priority: Optional[Priority] = None
    status: Optional[Status] = None
    due_date: Optional[date] = None
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = Field(None, min_items=0)

class TaskOut(TaskBase):
    id: int

    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

# Thread-safe in-memory storage
tasks: dict[int, TaskOut] = {}
next_id: int = 1
lock = threading.Lock()

def _get_next_id() -> int:
    global next_id
    with lock:
        nid = next_id
        next_id += 1
        return nid

def get_task(task_id: int) -> TaskOut:
    if task_id not in tasks:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Task not found")
    return tasks[task_id]

def parse_comma_list(s: Optional[str]) -> List[str]:
    return [x.strip() for x in (s or "").split(",") if x.strip()] if s else []

def get_tasks(
    limit: int = Query(20, ge=1, le=100),
    offset: int = Query(0, ge=0),
    status: Optional[str] = Query(None),
    priority: Optional[str] = Query(None),
    tags: Optional[str] = Query(None),
    sort_by: str = Query("id"),
    sort_dir: Literal["asc", "desc"] = Query("asc"),
    search: Optional[str] = Query("")
) -> List[TaskOut]:
    with lock:
        all_tasks = list(tasks.values())
    
    # Parse multi-filters (OR within category)
    statuses = parse_comma_list(status)
    priorities = parse_comma_list(priority)
    tags_list = parse_comma_list(tags)
    
    # Filter
    filtered = []
    for task in all_tasks:
        if statuses and task.status not in statuses:
            continue
        if priorities and task.priority not in priorities:
            continue
        if tags_list and not any(t in task.tags for t in tags_list):
            continue
        filtered.append(task)
    
    # Search (post-filter, case-insens substring on title/desc)
    if search:
        search_lower = search.lower()
        filtered = [t for t in filtered if search_lower in t.title.lower() or search_lower in t.description.lower()]
    
    # Sort key: handle any field, str-convert (None->'', list->','.join(sorted))
    def sort_key(task: TaskOut):
        val = getattr(task, sort_by, None)
        if val is None:
            return ""
        if isinstance(val, list):
            return ",".join(sorted(val))
        return str(val)
    
    reverse = sort_dir == "desc"
    filtered.sort(key=sort_key, reverse=reverse)

    priority: Priority
    status: Status
    due_date: Optional[date] = None
    tags: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list)

    @field_validator('tags')
    @classmethod
    def tags_non_empty(cls, v):
        if any((not isinstance(tag, str)) or (not tag.strip()) for tag in v):
            raise ValueError("Tags must be non-empty strings")
        return v

class TaskCreate(TaskBase):
    pass

class TaskUpdate(BaseModel):
    title: Optional[str] = Field(None, min_length=1)
    description: Optional[str] = Field(None, min_length=1)
    priority: Optional[Priority] = None
    status: Optional[Status] = None
    due_date: Optional[date] = None

    @field_validator('tags')

        if v is not None and any((not isinstance(tag, str)) or (not tag.strip()) for tag in v):
            raise ValueError("Tags must be non-empty strings")
        return v

class TaskOut(TaskBase):

class TaskStore:
    def __init__(self):

        self._next_id: int = 1
        self._lock = threading.Lock()

    def create(self, data: dict) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            tid = self._next_id
            self._next_id += 1
            task = {**data, "id": tid}
            self._store[tid] = task
            return task

    def get(self, tid: int) -> Optional[dict]:
        return self._store.get(tid)

    def update(self, tid: int, data: dict) -> Optional[dict]:
        if tid in self._store:
            self._store[tid].update(data)
            self._store[tid]["id"] = tid
            return self._store[tid]
        return None

    sort_by: str = Query("id", description="Field to sort by"),
    sort_dir: Literal["asc", "desc"] = Query("asc"),
    search: Optional[str] = Query(None, description="Case-insensitive substring in title/description"),
):
    tasks = tasks_db.all()
    # Filtering
    status_list = parse_csv_param(status)
    if status_list:
        tasks = [t for t in tasks if t["status"] in status_list]
    priority_list = parse_csv_param(priority)
    if priority_list:
        tasks = [t for t in tasks if t["priority"] in priority_list]
    tags_list = parse_csv_param(tags)
    if tags_list:
        tasks = [t for t in tasks if any(tag in t["tags"] for tag in tags_list)]
    # Search
    if search:
        s = search.lower()
        tasks = [t for t in tasks if s in t["title"].lower() or s in t["description"].lower()]
    # Sorting
    if not tasks:
        return []
    sample = tasks[0]
    allowed_fields = {"id", "title", "description", "priority", "status", "due_date", "tags"}
    if sort_by not in allowed_fields:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=f"Invalid sort_by field: {sort_by}")
    reverse = sort_dir == "desc"
    tasks.sort(key=lambda t: sort_key(t, sort_by), reverse=reverse)
    # Pagination
    return [TaskOut(**t) for t in tasks[offset:offset+limit]]

@app.get("/tasks/{task_id}", response_model=TaskOut)
def get_task(task_id: int):
    task = tasks_db.get(task_id)
    if not task:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Task not found")
    return TaskOut(**task)

@app.put("/tasks/{task_id}", response_model=TaskOut)
def update_task(task_id: int, update: TaskUpdate):
    task = tasks_db.get(task_id)
    if not task:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Task not found")
    update_data = update.model_dump(exclude_unset=True)
    if not update_data:
        return TaskOut(**task)
    # Validate tags
    if "tags" in update_data and update_data["tags"] is not None:
        TaskUpdate(tags=update_data["tags"])  # will raise if invalid
    updated = tasks_db.update(task_id, update_data)
    return TaskOut(**updated)

@app.delete("/tasks/{task_id}", status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)
def delete_task(task_id: int):
